critical.mandate: Quality gates must be enforced, not advisory

step.title: Detect CI Platform
step.action: Scan for existing CI config files: .github/workflows/, .gitlab-ci.yml, Jenkinsfile, .circleci/config.yml
If config found: note detected platform and present finding to user
If no config found: note that no existing CI platform was detected
step.ask: Which CI platform would you like to use? Options: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, CircleCI, or other. {If detected: "Detected: {platform} — recommended based on existing config."}

step.title: Preset Selection (Promotion Chain)

critical.mandate: This step configures the ci_cd.promotion_chain block in {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml. It uses the 4 canonical presets from {project-root}/_gaia/_config/environment-presets.yaml and the promotion-chain schema validator at {project-path}/test/validators/promotion-chain-validator.js.

step.action: Read {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml. Check whether a top-level ci_cd block exists (with or without a promotion_chain field).
step.check: Warn the user: "Existing ci_cd configuration found in global.yaml." and present three options: [o]verwrite — replace the existing ci_cd.promotion_chain with a newly selected preset (or custom chain) [s]kip — leave the existing ci_cd block unchanged and proceed to Step 3 (no write performed) [e]dit — redirect the user to /gaia-ci-edit for structured editing of the existing chain Respect the user's choice: - On [s]kip: jump directly to Step 3 without modifying global.yaml — backward compatible, no-op. - On [e]dit: hand off to /gaia-ci-edit and terminate this step. - On [o]verwrite: fall through to the preset-selection prompt below.
step.action: Read {project-root}/_gaia/_config/environment-presets.yaml — this framework config file contains the four canonical presets (solo, small-team, standard, enterprise). Each preset exposes a `description` field and a `promotion_chain` array.
step.check: HALT with error "Environment presets file not found at _gaia/_config/environment-presets.yaml. This file ships with the GAIA framework — your installation appears to be incomplete or outdated. Re-run the installer to restore missing config files: `gaia-install.sh update` (or `gaia-install.sh install` for a fresh install)." — this is the AC10/test-scenario-10 failure mode.
step.action: Parse the YAML and extract the 4 preset names: solo, small-team, standard, enterprise. For each preset, extract its `description` string to show to the user.
step.check: Auto-select the `standard` preset without prompting the user — this follows the existing YOLO convention of auto-answering from context when unambiguous. Log to the user: "YOLO mode: auto-selected `standard` preset (3-tier dev → staging → prod flow)." Skip the ask prompt below and proceed straight to the preset application action.
step.ask:
Select a promotion-chain preset for this project:
| # | Preset | Description | |---|--------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | 1 | solo | {presets.solo.description} — single environment, direct to main, auto-merge. | | 2 | small-team | {presets.small-team.description} — 2 envs (develop → main) for a small team with one pre-prod gate. | | 3 | standard | {presets.standard.description} — 3 envs (dev → staging → prod), the recommended default for product teams. | | 4 | enterprise | {presets.enterprise.description} — 4 envs (dev → uat → staging → prod) with approval gates and audit trail. | | 5 | custom | Define your own promotion_chain interactively (see Task 3 builder below). |
Choose [1] solo / [2] small-team / [3] standard / [4] enterprise / [5] custom:
step.check: Enter the interactive custom environment builder. For each environment, prompt the user for the following fields one-by-one, then loop until the user chooses to finish: Prompt for `id` (string, slug format [a-z0-9-]+, must be unique within the chain) — required. Prompt for `name` (human-readable string) — required. Prompt for `branch` (git branch name, must be unique within the chain) — required. Prompt for `ci_provider` — present enum selection: [1] github_actions [2] gitlab_ci [3] jenkins [4] circleci [5] azure_devops [6] bitbucket [7] none. Required. Prompt for `merge_strategy` — present enum selection: [1] merge [2] squash [3] rebase. Required unless a default is set in ci_cd.default_merge_strategy. Prompt for `ci_checks` — optional array of check names (e.g., lint, unit, integration, e2e). Accept empty to skip. Environment {n} defined. Add another environment? [y]es / [n]o (finish) When the user finishes, assemble the `promotion_chain` array from the collected entries. If the user added zero environments, warn them that the promotion chain requires a minimum of one environment and return to the loop.
step.action: If the user chose a named preset (solo/small-team/standard/enterprise): use the full promotion_chain array from the selected preset in environment-presets.yaml verbatim. If the user chose custom: use the array assembled by the custom builder above.

Load the current {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml content. Preserve all existing fields and comments — only add or replace the top-level `ci_cd` key. Merge semantics: write `ci_cd.promotion_chain: {selected array}` under the existing `ci_cd` block if one exists (from the overwrite path), or create a new `ci_cd` block containing only `promotion_chain` if none exists. All other global.yaml content (framework_version, user_name, project_path, planning_artifacts, etc.) MUST remain unchanged.
Write the updated content back to global.yaml. Do not reformat unrelated sections — emit a minimal diff focused on the ci_cd block.

After the write completes, invoke the promotion-chain schema validator at {project-path}/test/validators/promotion-chain-validator.js on the newly written block. The validator enforces: (a) minimum 1 environment (FR-252), (b) no duplicate ids across entries, (c) no duplicate branches across entries, (d) each entry has required fields id/name/branch/ci_provider, (e) ci_provider is one of the enum values, (f) merge_strategy (if set) is one of merge/squash/rebase, (g) id matches slug pattern [a-z0-9-]+.
step.check: Display the specific violation(s) returned by the validator — include each violation's field path and reason (e.g., "promotion_chain[1].id duplicates promotion_chain[0].id" or "promotion_chain is empty — minimum 1 environment required"). Prompt the user to correct the failing fields. After corrections, re-run the validator. Repeat this validate→correct loop until the block passes.
step.action: When validation passes, log success: "ci_cd.promotion_chain written to global.yaml and validated ({n} environment(s))."

step.title: Define Pipeline
step.action: Build > lint > test > coverage > deploy gates

step.title: Quality Gates
step.action: Define pass/fail thresholds: coverage %, test pass rate

step.title: Secrets Management
step.action: Identify all required secrets from architecture and PRD: API keys, database credentials, auth secrets, third-party service keys
Document how to add secrets to the selected CI platform (e.g., GitHub Repository Secrets, GitLab CI/CD Variables)
Define environment-level separation: which secrets differ between staging and production
List each secret with its name, purpose, and which pipeline stages need it

step.title: Deployment Strategy
step.action: Define staging deployment: auto-deploy on merge to develop branch after all gates pass
Define production deployment: deploy on merge to main with manual approval gate
Define rollback procedure: how to revert a failed deployment (rollback trigger, steps, verification)

step.title: Monitoring and Notifications
step.action: Configure pipeline failure notifications: Slack webhook, email, or platform-native alerts
Add pipeline status badge for the project README
Recommend metrics dashboard for pipeline health (run duration trends, failure rates, flaky test tracking)

step.title: Generate Pipeline Config
step.action: Generate CI config file (.github/workflows/ci.yml or equivalent)

step.title: Generate Output
step.action: Generate CI/CD pipeline configuration document with: pipeline stages, quality gates, secrets management, deployment strategy, and monitoring setup

next-step.primary: Validate implementation readiness before sprint planning
